claim: Switzerland's role as a countercase of sustained restrained-reordering is complicated by its wartime economic collaboration with Nazi Germany — the Swiss symbolic order's restraint was partially purchased by accommodation with an adjacent sacrificial-escalation regime, raising questions about whether the restraint was principled or strategic.
rationale: A countercase argument requires that the symbolic order's restraint mechanisms be causally prior to the outcome; if Swiss restraint was partly enabled by economic benefit from Nazi Germany, the causal story is more complex than the standard countercase framing suggests.
